source_pr.title: *: fix unstable test TestIndexDoubleReadClose
source_pr.body:
<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and 
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->

Issue Number: close #45245

Problem Summary:

### What is changed and how it works?

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [ ] Unit test
- [ ] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No code

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

```release-note
None
```

symptoms: After closing the result set, a background goroutine (e.g., `pickAndExecTask`) could still be running, and the test's fixed sleep was sometimes insufficient before asserting the goroutine had exited (executor/test/seqtest/seq_executor_test.go).
root_cause_explanation: The test asserted that a background worker goroutine was gone right after closing the result set, but goroutine shutdown is asynchronous. A fixed `time.Sleep(10ms)` could be too short on slower runs, making the goroutine-existence check flaky. The fix replaces the fixed sleep with an `Eventually` loop that waits until the goroutine disappears (within a bounded timeout), making the test robust to timing variability (executor/test/seqtest/seq_executor_test.go).
fix_pattern: Replace fixed sleeps with `require.Eventually` (poll/wait until goroutines exit) when asserting asynchronous cleanup after Close().
